case_title: S. Nagaraj (dead) by LRs. & Ors. v. B.R. Vasudeva & Ors. Etc. Etc.

parties.appellants: S. Nagaraj (dead) by LRs. & Ors., Bangalore Development Authority, Teachers' Colony Residents Association

judgment_summary: The Supreme Court set aside the Karnataka High Court's common judgment dated 22.12.2006, ruling that the previous findings of the High Court in 1998 had become final and binding. The Court held that the Inamdars had waived their rights to the land by entering into a settlement agreement and accepting compensation. Consequently, the Minister of Revenue's 2003 order, which attempted to resume land and restore vacant sites to the Inamdars, was declared to be without the authority of law and was set aside.
key_directions: The impugned common judgment dated 22.12.2006 of the Karnataka High Court is set aside[cite: 376].
The writ petitions filed before the Karnataka High Court (challenging the Minister's 2003 order) are allowed[cite: 376].
The directions in the Minister's order dated 22.12.2003 for cancellation of the grant to the Sangha and transfer of 182 sites to Inamdars are set aside[cite: 375].
Directions to the BDA to hand over civic amenity sites to Inamdars free of cost and pay compensation for the ring road are set aside[cite: 375].
Civil Appeal No. 1480 of 2010 is remanded to the City Civil Court, Bangalore, to decide the application for temporary injunction and the suit in accordance with this judgment[cite: 411, 412].
action_type: Judgment outcome finalized; Minister's restoration order quashed; High Court judgment set aside

appeal_timeline.label: Estimated review/curative petition deadline
appeal_timeline.note: This is a final judgment from the Supreme Court; timeline inferred for standard procedural finality/review purposes.
